Primary_Networks_Disrupted: Basal ganglia-thalamocortical loop (impaired inhibition — excess pallidal output), Sensorimotor network (abnormal plasticity — maladaptive LTP), Cerebellar circuit
Network_Dysfunction_Pattern: GPi excessive inhibitory output → thalamic suppression → inadequate cortical brake on aberrant movements; maladaptive sensorimotor plasticity → fixed dystonic patterns; ↓ inhibitory surround in M1
Recommended_Neuromod_Techniques: DBS GPi 130 Hz; TMS SMA/premotor cortex; tDCS cathodal M1
Primary_Stimulation_Targets: GPi (DBS 130 Hz), SMA (FCz), premotor cortex, M1 (cathodal)
Stimulation_Rationale: GPi DBS normalizes excessive pallidal output → restores thalamocortical drive balance; cathodal M1 ↓ cortical excitability to reduce dystonic overflow; SMA TMS disrupts maladaptive motor programs

Affected_Brain_Regions: Widespread white matter lesions, corpus callosum, periventricular areas, brainstem, cerebellum, optic nerves
Primary_Networks_Disrupted: Sensorimotor network (disrupted by demyelination), Executive network (cognitive fatigue), Visual network (optic neuritis), Global connectivity ↓
Network_Dysfunction_Pattern: Demyelination → slowed conduction velocity → ↑ theta/delta, ↓ alpha; corpus callosum lesions → ↓ interhemispheric coherence; frontal theta excess correlates directly with cognitive fatigue severity
Recommended_Neuromod_Techniques: PEMF; tRNS 2 mA M1 (pain/spasticity); TMS; tDCS

Key_Symptoms: Widespread pain, tenderness, fatigue, cognitive dysfunction (fibro-fog), sleep disturbance, headaches, IBS, anxiety, depression
qEEG_Patterns: ↑ theta power (concentrated slowing), ↑ theta/alpha + theta/beta ratios, ↓ delta (R insula), ↑ beta (R middle frontal, midcingulate), ↓ alpha peak frequency, ↓ EO/EC reactivity, ↑ centro-parietal theta/beta coherence
Key_qEEG_Electrode_Sites: Cz, C3/C4 (central theta), Fz (frontal slowing), Pz, P3/P4 (parietal theta), T3/T4 (temporal), O1/O2 (alpha peak)
Affected_Brain_Regions: Thalamus (↓ activity), PFC (grey matter loss), ACC, insula, M1, S1, midcingulate
Primary_Networks_Disrupted: Pain matrix/Salience (sensitized — central sensitization), DMN (disrupted), Thalamocortical dysrhythmia circuit, Descending pain modulation (impaired)
Network_Dysfunction_Pattern: Central sensitization — thalamocortical dysrhythmia → widespread pain amplification; descending PAG modulation impaired → no pain gating; insula ↓ delta paradoxically → ↑ pain interoception

Stimulation_Rationale: M1 tDCS activates descending corticothalamic pain inhibition; DLPFC targets cognitive/affective pain component and fibro-fog; CES/taVNS normalizes autonomic imbalance and ↓ central sensitization

Key_qEEG_Electrode_Sites: Fz, F3/F4 (frontal changes), Cz (central), T3/T4 (temporal), P3/P4 (parietal), globally altered
Affected_Brain_Regions: PFC, temporal cortex, fusiform gyrus (face processing), amygdala, cerebellum, STS (superior temporal sulcus)
Primary_Networks_Disrupted: Social brain network (impaired — STS, fusiform, amygdala, mPFC), DMN (atypical connectivity), Mirror neuron network (altered), Long-range underconnectivity + local overconnectivity pattern
Network_Dysfunction_Pattern: Long-range underconnectivity → poor social brain network integration; local overconnectivity → restricted/repetitive processing; ↓ alpha in social cortex → ↓ inhibitory gating of sensory input → sensory sensitivity
Recommended_Neuromod_Techniques: TMS DLPFC/SMA; TPS (emerging); tDCS DLPFC; neurofeedback

Key_Symptoms: Impaired awareness, reduced/absent responsiveness, vegetative state, minimally conscious state, coma, fluctuating awareness
qEEG_Patterns: Generalized delta/theta dominance; absent/severely ↓ alpha/beta; loss of normal sleep architecture; ↓ EEG complexity/entropy; absent posterior dominant rhythm; ↓ reactivity to stimulation; theta patterns may indicate minimal consciousness
Key_qEEG_Electrode_Sites: Global (all channels), Fz/Cz/Pz (midline), C3/C4, O1/O2
Affected_Brain_Regions: Thalamus (intralaminar nuclei, CL, CM-Pf), brainstem reticular formation, PFC, precuneus, posterior cingulate
Primary_Networks_Disrupted: ARAS/ascending arousal system (severely impaired), Thalamocortical system (disconnected), DMN (disrupted — correlates with consciousness level), Frontoparietal network (connectivity predicts recovery)
Network_Dysfunction_Pattern: ARAS-thalamic disconnection → absent arousal drive → suppressed cortical EEG; thalamocortical relay failure → delta dominance; frontoparietal connectivity level correlates with degree of awareness

Stimulation_Rationale: Intralaminar thalamic DBS restores thalamocortical arousal drive; L-DLPFC excitation from non-invasive devices attempts to re-engage frontoparietal consciousness network; LIFU deep thalamic targeting is minimally invasive
